context.docs: CLAUDE.md (living source of truth — overrides the spec on conflict) + CDP-BUILD-SPEC.md §9B (automation workflow engine), §8 (rule-AST→SQL compiler, reused for branch conditions), §9 (outbox→Dispatcher pipeline)
env: Monorepo: pnpm workspaces + Turborepo + Vitest + strict TS. Run from repo root. Node 20+.

Real Postgres (dev): DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp (migrations applied). Single test file: pnpm --filter <pkg> exec vitest run path. Whole suite: DATABASE_URL=… pnpm test. Typecheck: pnpm typecheck. Lint: pnpm lint.

Browser e2e: pnpm --filter @cdp/web test:e2e — its OWN DB cdp_e2e on its OWN ports (local-api :8788, web :5174), runs with LOCAL_SES_FORCE_MOCK=1 LOCAL_SES_DKIM_STATUS=SUCCESS; re-seeds web/e2e/seed.ts each run. The dev stack (cdp, :8787/:5173) coexists. Playwright config has retries:1.
Dev API for manual checks: pnpm --filter @cdp/service-local-api dev:api (background, restart after backend edits). Health: GET http://localhost:8787/health.

Key reuse points: services/automation-runner (dsl.ts, core.ts, run.ts, enroll.ts), services/local-api (handlers.ts, routes.ts, app.ts, server.ts), services/dispatcher (core.ts, dispatch.ts), packages/segments (AST + compiler + rule builder), packages/email, web/src/screens (BroadcastComposer email-instance flow, TemplateEditor, SegmentBuilder), web/src/ui/kit.tsx (ActionMenu, Drawer, Button, Field, Select), web/e2e/seed.ts.
invariants: Tenant isolation: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL and every query filters by workspace_id. The automation-runner + enrollment + handlers connect with the Supabase SERVICE ROLE which BYPASSES RLS — they MUST scope by workspace_id IN CODE (RLS is not their safety net). workspace_id is loaded FROM the enrollment/automation row, never assumed.

workspace_id is NEVER client-supplied — it comes from the authorizer-injected claim (admin API) or a trusted server-set field (the enrollment row). Never trust a workspace_id in a request body. Reject a cross-workspace template_id / segment_id / sender_id (inv.2) — like validateSenderId does today.

All automation action SENDS go through the SAME outbox → Dispatcher pipeline as broadcasts (§9): suppression → frequency-cap → quiet-hours, gated on a VERIFIED sending domain. A send node carries an email INSTANCE whose envelope (real named From sender + To + Subject) is ALL mandatory — NO no-reply fallback (mirror sendBroadcast gating). The Dispatcher renders merge tags in the SUBJECT, To AND body.

Runner idempotency + concurrency (unchanged guarantee): one tick advances a due enrollment exactly once (SELECT … FOR UPDATE row lock in one tx, re-check active inside the lock; legacy CAS path for unit tests). Outbox dedupe_key per (automation_id, profile_id, node_id) prevents double-sends on retry. MAX_STEPS_PER_TICK guards pathological graphs. New node execution must preserve all of this.

Webhook action safety: outbound HTTP ONLY to an explicit per-workspace domain ALLOWLIST; enforce a timeout + BOUNDED retries; block SSRF (no internal/link-local/loopback/169.254 metadata targets); secrets (e.g. auth headers) use the @cdp/db secret-crypto envelope pattern, never returned in plaintext over the API; a webhook failure is ISOLATED (recorded; never crashes the tick or double-advances). LOCAL: the HTTP call is mocked/injected — never hit a real external host in tests.

Workspace timezone governs ALL automation time math (waits, wait-until, hour-of-day window) — DST-correct (reuse the zoned↔UTC approach from the broadcast scheduler: zonedInputToUtcIso/tzOffsetMs). One configurable timezone per workspace; never per-broadcast guesswork.

The DSL ({startNode, nodes}) is the SINGLE graph model; validateAutomationDefinition is the structural gate (exactly ONE trigger, every edge resolves, a reachable exit, and — extended — NO cycles / NO orphan nodes / down-only). The builder is an AUTO-LAYOUT RENDER of that graph: compute (x,y) from the tree, draw rounded orthogonal connectors, branches fan sideways. NO separate graph model, NO stored coordinates, NO loops/back-edges.

REUSE, do not reinvent (code-quality bar): the broadcast email-INSTANCE/clone flow (kind=copy, source_template_id, cloneTemplate, the From/To/Subject envelope + editor return flow) for send nodes; the segment rule builder + AstNode for the if/condition node; the kit ActionMenu / Drawer / Button (auto-locks on promise-returning onClick) / Field / Select; the Dispatcher core; scopedQuery; runInWorkspaceTx; evaluateRealtimeSegmentsForProfile. Match the surrounding code style, comment density and naming.

UI standing rules: NEVER use native confirm/alert/prompt — use ui/dialog.tsx (askConfirm) or purpose-built modals. EVERY server-calling button shows a spinner + locks until the response (kit Button auto-locks when onClick RETURNS the promise — wire it that way). The email editor autosaves (no manual save). PRESERVE every existing data-testid; add stable testids for new UI. Floating toasts (ui/toast.tsx showToast) for transient feedback.

Test-first (§16A/§18): write the failing acceptance tests FIRST, then implement to green. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier — use the REAL local DB. Mock SES/AWS (aws-sdk-client-mock / LocalStack); the webhook HTTP client is injected/mocked. Playwright (real browser) for UI phases with seeded data + stable selectors. turbo test task is cache:false — keep it. Unique workspace UUIDs + file-local namespaces (events.event_id / ses_message_id are GLOBAL keys).

Process hygiene: BUMP the root package.json version per change (patch=tiny, minor=feature, major only when told). UPDATE CLAUDE.md when behavior/architecture changes. Apply new SQL migrations to BOTH the dev DB (cdp) AND the e2e DB (cdp_e2e) via a node pg script (psql is unavailable). RESTART the dev API (pnpm --filter @cdp/service-local-api dev:api — a long-lived tsx process, NOT watch) after backend edits, and REBUILD a changed package (tsc -b) the local-api imports from dist. Scope discipline: build EXACTLY the agreed design — no extra features.
context.loop: Backend phase: pure unit tests + DB-integration against REAL local Postgres (DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp). Mock AWS/SES (aws-sdk-client-mock) / LocalStack; the webhook HTTP client is injected/mocked. No frontend.

criteria: Segment-entry enrollment still works (no regression). Event ingestion of the configured type enrolls the profile into the active automation exactly once (idempotent on replay); a non-matching event/filter does NOT enroll.

The manual/API enroll endpoint enrolls a profile (and a segment snapshot) at the start node, is capability-gated + workspace-scoped, and refuses a cross-workspace automation/profile/segment id (404/inv.2).
The re-enrollment policy is documented + enforced for all three trigger kinds. All verified against REAL Postgres; everything workspace-scoped.
phase.refs: §9B (enrollment), CLAUDE.md (segment_change_log → enrollFromSegmentChange; ingest/processor), the re-enrollment policy
scope: Persist + validate the TRIGGER definition for all three kinds: segment_entry (automations.trigger_segment_id, already wired via enrollFromSegmentChange), event (event type + optional payload filter — store in the trigger node/definition), manual (no auto-source).

EVENT-trigger enrollment: wire processor-time enrollment — when an event of the configured type (matching the optional filter) is ingested for a profile, enroll into the matching active automations (insert automation_enrollments at the start node, UNIQUE(automation_id, profile_id), ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, workspace-scoped). Idempotent (a replayed event enrolls at most once).

MANUAL/API enrollment: a workspace-scoped endpoint (e.g. POST /automations/:id/enroll) to enroll a single profile or a segment snapshot; capability-gated (manage_content); rejects cross-workspace ids (inv.2). Define + enforce the re-enrollment policy (once vs re-enter) consistently across all three kinds.
phase.title: Triggers & enrollment: segment-entry + event + manual

implUnits: 1. dsl.ts: extend TriggerNode for kind:'event' with `eventType: string` (required) + optional `filter?: AstNode` payload filter; manual stays {kind,next}; segment_entry unchanged. Extend validateNodeFields trigger case to require eventType for event triggers and to structurally check an optional filter. (No migration — event config lives in definition JSON.)

2. core.ts: add parseEventEnrollmentTrigger (event analogue of parseEnrollmentTrigger) + evaluateEventPayloadFilter (pure filter eval). Reuse decideReenrollment + buildEnrollmentInsert unchanged. Add an EventRow type (workspace_id, profile_id, type, payload, event_id).

3. enroll.ts: add enrollFromEvent(deps, eventRow) — reads active automations in the event's workspace whose definition has an event trigger matching the type (load definition, validate, resolveStartNode, read eventType+filter from the trigger node), evaluate the optional filter against the payload, build intents (parseEventEnrollmentTrigger), and apply buildEnrollmentInsert statements in ONE workspace-scoped tx — mirrors enrollFromSegmentChange exactly (in-code workspace scoping, ON CONFLICT 'once').

4. enroll.ts: add enrollProfileManually(deps, {workspaceId, automationId, profileId}) and enrollSegmentSnapshot(deps, {workspaceId, automationId, segmentId}) — resolve startNode from the automation's validated definition; the segment snapshot resolves current members (dynamic via @cdp/segments buildSegmentMatch/compileWhere; manual via segment_memberships) and inserts one enrollment per member; all workspace-scoped, ON CONFLICT 'once'.
5. index.ts: export enrollFromEvent, enrollProfileManually, enrollSegmentSnapshot, parseEventEnrollmentTrigger, evaluateEventPayloadFilter, and the new EventRow/TriggerNode types.

6. local-api handlers.ts: add enrollIntoAutomation handler for POST /automations/:id/enroll — validate body (exactly one of profile_id|segment_id), resolve the automation scoped to ctx.workspaceId (404 if absent), verify the profile/segment exists in ctx.workspaceId (404 cross-workspace), then call the automation-runner enroll core via an injected Reader+runInWorkspaceTx bound to the request pool (reuse the recomputeFeaturesAndSegments dependency-wiring style). Return enrolled count.

7. local-api handlers.ts: wire EVENT-trigger enrollment into the event landing path. In sendProfileEvent (and document the prod processor equivalent in services/processor/src/deps.ts.runSegmentReevalInTx neighbor), AFTER the event row is written + recomputeFeaturesAndSegments, call enrollFromEvent with the just-recorded event (type+payload+profile) on the SAME tx client. This is the local hook point that mirrors the processor; keep it idempotent and non-throwing.
8. local-api routes.ts: register 'POST /automations/:id/enroll' : 'manage_content'.
9. local-api handlers.ts handler map: add 'POST /automations/:id/enroll' : enrollIntoAutomation.

10. CLAUDE.md: document the re-enrollment policy (default 'once' across all three kinds; structural ON CONFLICT(automation_id,profile_id) guard) + the event-trigger config location (definition trigger node: eventType + optional payload filter) + the new endpoint. Bump root package.json (minor — feature).

11. (no migration) — if a 'once'/'always' policy ever needs persistence it would be a automations column; THIS phase keeps the default 'once' in code (decideReenrollment) so NO migration. If the design review insists on persisting the policy, add a automations.reenrollment_policy column and apply to BOTH cdp AND cdp_e2e via a node pg script (psql unavailable) — flagged, not assumed.
mocks: NONE for the DB tier — integration tests use REAL local Postgres (DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp) via adminPool()/hasDatabaseUrl(), with describe.skipIf(!RUN) and unique per-file workspace/automation/segment/profile UUID namespaces (parity with enrollment-from-segment-entry.integration.test.ts).
NO SES/SQS/Dispatcher in this phase — enrollment writes automation_enrollments only; it does NOT enqueue sends, so no aws-sdk-client-mock / LocalStack needed here.
Reader + runInWorkspaceTx are INJECTED (EnrollDeps), bound to the test's admin pool via runStatementsInWorkspaceTx — same dependency-injection seam the segment-entry test uses; no Postgres mock.
The local-api endpoint test drives the handler through the real app/router with a manage_content ctx; no AWS mocks.
plan.notes: WHERE EVENT-TRIGGER ENROLLMENT HOOKS IN: events land in the events table in two places that share semantics — (a) the production processor (services/processor: planProcessing → runPlanInWorkspaceTx, which after the event/feature upsert runs runSegmentReevalInTx on the open tx client), and (b) the local dev path POST /profiles/:id/events (sendProfileEvent → recomputeFeaturesAndSegments) which is the dev mirror (the local-api has no SQS/processor). The event-trigger enroll call (enrollFromEvent) should fire AT THE SAME POINT the segment re-eval fires — right after the event row + feature recompute, on the SAME workspace-scoped tx client (no nested BEGIN/COMMIT). For the dev tier that's inside sendProfileEvent; for prod it's the processor deps tx. IMPORTANT: enrollFromSegmentChange is currently EXPORTED but NOT yet wired into any live flow (only covered by tests) — so the segment-entry trigger is materialized but not auto-invoked at processor time today; this phase should NOT regress that (keep its tests green) and may optionally wire it alongside the event hook, but the criteria only require event + manual to be wired. RE-ENROLLMENT POLICY (recommended): default 'once' for ALL THREE kinds, enforced structurally by UNIQUE(automation_id, profile_id) + INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (buildEnrollmentInsert) and decided in code by decideReenrollment(hasExisting,'once'); 'always' (re-entry) stays a forward-compat enum value but is NOT enabled this phase. This is consistent because segment_entry, event, and manual ALL funnel through buildEnrollmentInsert. MIGRATION: NONE required — the event trigger's eventType + optional payload filter live in the automation definition JSON (the TriggerNode), and the 'once' policy is a code default; automation_enrollments already has UNIQUE(automation_id, profile_id). If a future design review wants the policy persisted per-automation, that single column (automations.reenrollment_policy default 'once') is the only migration and MUST be applied to BOTH cdp and cdp_e2e via a node pg script (psql unavailable) — flagged but out of scope. TENANT ISOLATION: every read filters WHERE workspace_id=$1 and every enrollment insert binds workspace_id at $1 (service role bypasses RLS); the endpoint takes workspace_id from the authorizer ctx ONLY and refuses cross-workspace automation/profile/segment ids with 404 (inv.2). The manual-enroll SEGMENT SNAPSHOT is point-in-time: resolve current members at call time (dynamic via buildSegmentMatch, manual via segment_memberships) and insert one enrollment each — later segment changes do not retroactively enroll. No sends are emitted in this phase (enrollment only), so no SES/SQS mocking needed. Follow process hygiene: bump root package.json (minor), update CLAUDE.md, restart dev API + rebuild changed packages after edits.
pureFunctions: parseEventEnrollmentTrigger(eventRow, automationTriggerRows) → EnrollmentIntent[] — event-kind analogue of parseEnrollmentTrigger; matches eventType, filters by workspace_id, drops intents whose payload-filter result is false (added to services/automation-runner/src/core.ts).

evaluateEventPayloadFilter(filter | undefined, payload) → boolean — pure, in-memory, closed-grammar evaluation of the optional event trigger filter against the event payload (payload.* namespace; reuses @cdp/segments operator/field whitelist conventions; no DB, never interpolated). In core.ts or a small filter module.
decideReenrollment(hasExisting, policy='once') → boolean — ALREADY EXISTS in core.ts; reused unchanged as the single re-enrollment decision for all three kinds.
buildEnrollmentInsert(ws, automation, profile, startNode) → SqlStatement — ALREADY EXISTS; the structural ON CONFLICT 'once' guard shared by segment_entry / event / manual.

validateAutomationDefinition (extended) — add event-trigger field validation (required eventType string; optional well-formed payload-filter AstNode) inside the existing trigger case of validateNodeFields in dsl.ts; no new public function.
resolveStartNode / parseEnrollmentTrigger — ALREADY EXIST; reused by the event + manual enroll orchestrators to compute startNode and shape intents.
reuse: enrollFromSegmentChange (enroll.ts) — the EXACT pattern to extend for enrollFromEvent: read active automations scoped to the workspace, validate+resolveStartNode, build intents, apply in one workspace-scoped tx. Same EnrollDeps { reader, runInWorkspaceTx } seam.
parseEnrollmentTrigger + EnrollmentIntent + AutomationTriggerRow (core.ts) — copy the shape for parseEventEnrollmentTrigger.
decideReenrollment + buildEnrollmentInsert (core.ts) — the single re-enrollment decision + structural ON CONFLICT 'once' insert, shared verbatim across all three trigger kinds.
validateAutomationDefinition / validateNodeFields / resolveStartNode (dsl.ts) — extend the trigger case; reuse the structural gate so the event fields ride the existing cycle/orphan/edge checks.

@cdp/segments buildSegmentMatch + compileWhere (statements.ts/compile.ts) — resolve the manual-enroll SEGMENT SNAPSHOT members (dynamic) parameterized with workspace_id at $1; segment_memberships query for manual segments (parity with listProfiles' segment branch in handlers.ts).
@cdp/segments AstNode + operator/field whitelist conventions — reuse for the event payload filter grammar (payload.* namespace) so it is compiled/whitelisted, never interpolated (invariant 6 spirit).

runStatementsInWorkspaceTx / withWorkspaceTx (automation-runner deps.ts) — the workspace-scoped tx runner the integration tests bind to adminPool(); asserts each statement is scoped to the requested workspace.

sendProfileEvent + recomputeFeaturesAndSegments (local-api handlers.ts) — the event landing path + the in-tx injected {reader, runInWorkspaceTx} wiring; the event-trigger enroll hook plugs in right after, on the same client.
scopedQuery (@cdp/db) + the validateSenderId cross-workspace-refusal pattern (handlers.ts) — for the endpoint's workspace scoping and 404-on-foreign-id behavior.
adminPool()/hasDatabaseUrl()/describe.skipIf + unique-UUID-namespace harness (enrollment-from-segment-entry.integration.test.ts) — the integration test scaffold to copy.

assertions: validateAutomationDefinition ACCEPTS a definition whose single trigger is { type:'trigger', kind:'event', eventType:'purchase', next:'x' } with no filter.
validateAutomationDefinition ACCEPTS an event trigger with an optional payload filter (an AstNode / rule object) and a valid eventType string.
validateAutomationDefinition THROWS when kind:'event' has a missing/empty eventType (event triggers MUST name the event type).
validateAutomationDefinition THROWS when the optional event-trigger filter is present but malformed (not an object / not a valid AstNode shape).

validateAutomationDefinition ACCEPTS kind:'manual' with only { kind, next } (no auto-source fields) and ACCEPTS kind:'segment_entry' unchanged (segment lives in automations.trigger_segment_id, NOT on the node) — segment_entry regression guard.

the exactly-ONE-trigger / resolvable-edges / no-cycle / no-orphan invariants are unaffected by the new event fields (a definition that was valid before stays valid; reuses existing detectCycle/hasReachableExit/findOrphans paths).
testFiles.criterion: Persist + validate the trigger definition for all three kinds (segment_entry | event | manual). The event trigger node must carry an event type + optional payload filter; manual carries no auto-source; the structural gate (validateAutomationDefinition) still passes/fails correctly.

assertions: parseEventEnrollmentTrigger(eventRow, automationTriggerRows) returns one EnrollmentIntent per active automation whose event trigger's eventType === the event's type (same workspace) — mirrors parseEnrollmentTrigger's shape (workspaceId/automationId/profileId/startNode).
an event type that matches NO automation's eventType yields zero intents.
a automation whose workspace_id !== the event row's workspace_id is filtered out (in-code tenant isolation; never enrolls cross-workspace).
when the event trigger has NO payload filter, a matching-type event always produces an intent.

when the event trigger HAS a payload filter, the intent is produced ONLY when matchesFilter===true is fed in (parse layer takes the boolean; the actual SQL match is the integration test's job) — a false filter result drops the intent.
THROWS on a falsy workspace_id (tenant-isolation guard, parity with parseEnrollmentTrigger).
testFiles.criterion: Event ingestion of the configured type (matching an optional payload filter) enrolls the profile into matching active automations exactly once; a non-matching event/filter does NOT enroll. (Pure parse layer — the SQL/idempotency is exercised in the integration test.)

assertions: evaluateEventPayloadFilter(undefined, anyPayload) === true (no filter ⇒ always matches).
a simple equality rule (e.g. payload.plan == 'pro') returns true for { plan:'pro' } and false for { plan:'free' } / a missing key.
numeric comparison (e.g. payload.amount >= 100) honors >= / < etc. against the event payload values.

an unknown/unsupported operator or a non-whitelisted path is REJECTED (throws) rather than silently matching — the filter is a closed grammar (reuses the @cdp/segments operator/field whitelist conventions; payload.* is the namespace).
the function is PURE and in-memory over the event payload object (no DB) so it is unit-testable without Postgres.
testFiles.criterion: The optional payload filter is evaluated against the INGESTED EVENT (not the profile), deterministically and safely (whitelist/parameterized — never interpolated).

assertions: decideReenrollment(hasExisting=false, 'once') === true; decideReenrollment(true, 'once') === false (default 'once').
decideReenrollment(true, 'always') === true; decideReenrollment(false, 'always') === true.

buildEnrollmentInsert always emits ON CONFLICT (automation_id, profile_id) DO NOTHING — the structural 'once' guard is identical regardless of trigger kind (segment_entry / event / manual all go through buildEnrollmentInsert).

the policy default is 'once' for ALL three kinds (assert the resolver/default used by enrollFromSegmentChange, the event-enroll path, and the manual-enroll path is the same decideReenrollment('once')).
testFiles.criterion: The re-enrollment policy is documented + enforced consistently across all three trigger kinds (decideReenrollment is the single decision point).

assertions: Given an ACTIVE automation with an event trigger (eventType='purchase', no filter), calling enrollFromEvent(deps, {workspace_id, profile_id, type:'purchase', payload, event_id}) inserts ONE automation_enrollments row at the start node with status='active' (current_node === definition.startNode).
REPLAY idempotency: invoking enrollFromEvent twice for the same (automation, profile) leaves exactly ONE enrollment row (ON CONFLICT (automation_id, profile_id) DO NOTHING) — count === 1.
an event of a DIFFERENT type than the automation's eventType enrolls NOBODY (0 rows for a fresh profile).

with a payload filter (payload.amount >= 100): a 'purchase' with amount=150 enrolls; a 'purchase' with amount=50 does NOT enroll (filter evaluated against the event payload; SQL/in-memory parameterized, never interpolated).

TENANT ISOLATION: a automation in workspace B with the same eventType is NOT enrolled when the event row belongs to workspace A; only workspace-A automations are read (WHERE workspace_id=$1) and every write binds workspace_id at $1.
an event trigger automation whose status != 'active' (draft/paused) does NOT enroll.
SEGMENT-ENTRY REGRESSION: in the SAME suite (or a shared harness), enrollFromSegmentChange on an 'entered' row still enrolls exactly once — assert the segment-entry path is untouched.

all three kinds share buildEnrollmentInsert → a profile already enrolled via segment_entry is NOT re-enrolled by a later matching event (single UNIQUE(automation_id,profile_id) row), proving the 'once' policy is consistent across kinds.
testFiles.criterion: Event ingestion of the configured type (matching the optional payload filter) enrolls the profile into matching active automations EXACTLY once (idempotent on replay); a non-matching event/filter does NOT enroll; segment-entry still works (no regression). Real Postgres, workspace-scoped.

assertions: Recording an event via the production processor-style path (the event row written + the post-event hook) invokes enrollFromEvent and produces the enrollment — proving the hook fires after the event lands (events row exists AND enrollment row exists).
the hook runs in/with the SAME workspace-scoped tx semantics as the existing feature/segment re-eval (no nested BEGIN/COMMIT against an open client; statements bind workspace_id at $1).

a replayed event_id (ON CONFLICT(event_id) DO NOTHING in the event insert) does NOT cause a second enrollment — the enroll hook is idempotent even when the underlying event insert was a no-op (assert enrollment count stays 1).
an event that matches no event-trigger automation leaves automation_enrollments empty (hook is a no-op, never throws, never blocks the event write).
testFiles.criterion: Event-trigger enrollment is wired at processor/ingest time: when an event of the configured type is recorded for a profile (the SAME code path POST /profiles/:id/events and the processor use), the profile is enrolled into matching active automations in the same workspace-scoped flow. Real Postgres.

assertions: POST /automations/:id/enroll { profile_id } enrolls that profile at the automation's start node (current_node === startNode, status 'active'); response reports enrolled count 1.

POST /automations/:id/enroll { segment_id } enrolls a SNAPSHOT of the segment's current members (each at the start node) — assert N enrollments for an N-member segment; the snapshot is resolved server-side (dynamic via compileWhere/buildSegmentMatch, manual via segment_memberships) and is point-in-time (a profile added to the segment AFTER the call is NOT enrolled).
IDEMPOTENT: a second identical enroll call inserts no duplicates (ON CONFLICT (automation_id, profile_id) DO NOTHING) — re-enroll a 3-member snapshot ⇒ still 3 rows.
CROSS-WORKSPACE automation id (a automation in another workspace): 404 (scopedQuery resolves nothing in the token's workspace) — no enrollment written.
CROSS-WORKSPACE profile_id: 404/refused (the profile must exist in ctx.workspaceId, mirrors sendProfileEvent's present-check) — never enrolls another tenant's profile.
CROSS-WORKSPACE segment_id: 404/refused (segment resolved scoped to ctx.workspaceId; a foreign id resolves to nothing) — inv.2.
workspace_id is taken from ctx (authorizer claim) ONLY — a workspace_id in the request body is ignored (assert a body workspace_id does not leak into the write).
CAPABILITY GATE: the route is registered as 'POST /automations/:id/enroll' : 'manage_content' in routes.ts (assert the entry exists / a non-manage_content token is rejected by the gate).
the inserted enrollment's workspace_id === ctx.workspaceId and automation_id === the path id (every write binds workspace_id at $1).
testFiles.criterion: The manual/API enroll endpoint (POST /automations/:id/enroll) enrolls a single profile OR a segment snapshot at the start node, is capability-gated (manage_content) + workspace-scoped, and refuses cross-workspace automation/profile/segment ids (404/inv.2). Real Postgres.

assertions: missing both profile_id and segment_id ⇒ 400 (a target is required).
both profile_id AND segment_id supplied ⇒ 400 (ambiguous; choose one).
a non-string/blank profile_id or segment_id ⇒ 400.
the handler RETURNS the promise so the kit Button auto-lock contract holds (no implementation detail leaked — assert via the handler signature/return, consistent with existing handlers).
testFiles.criterion: The manual-enroll handler input contract: exactly one of profile_id | segment_id required; bad input rejected before any DB write.

instructions: Read CLAUDE.md (living source of truth — overrides the spec on conflict) + CDP-BUILD-SPEC.md §9B (automation workflow engine), §8 (rule-AST→SQL compiler, reused for branch conditions), §9 (outbox→Dispatcher pipeline) + the cited files: §9B (enrollment), CLAUDE.md (segment_change_log → enrollFromSegmentChange; ingest/processor), the re-enrollment policy. Reuse the existing automation-runner / dispatcher / segment compiler / broadcast email-instance flow / UI kit per the plan's "reuse" list.
FIRST write the failing tests from the plan (red), then implement to green. Logic in pure injected functions; handlers/components thin.

Honor every invariant — tenant isolation, workspace_id never client-supplied, all sends through the Dispatcher gating, runner idempotency/concurrency, webhook SSRF/allowlist + injected HTTP, workspace-tz time math, the single DSL graph model (no stored coords/loops/orphans), reuse + UI standing rules.

Mock AWS/SES (aws-sdk-client-mock) or LocalStack; the integration tier uses the REAL local Postgres (never mock the DB). The webhook HTTP client is injected/mocked. For UI phases verify with Playwright (real browser) against the e2e stack; seed deterministic data.

Apply any new SQL migration to BOTH cdp AND cdp_e2e (node pg script; psql unavailable). Run the relevant pnpm test (with DATABASE_URL) + pnpm typecheck + pnpm lint, and Playwright e2e for UI phases. Rebuild a changed package (tsc -b) and RESTART the dev API if you manually exercise it. Iterate until GREEN incl. a COLD run; confirm no regression. Report actual command output.
Bump the root package.json version appropriately and update CLAUDE.md if behavior/architecture changed. Implement ONLY the agreed design — no scope creep. Return ONLY the JSON.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { filesCreated: string[], filesModified: string[], migrationsApplied: string[], versionBump: string, testsPass: boolean, testSummary: string, e2eSummary: string, typecheckOk: boolean, lintOk: boolean, commandsRun: string[], notes: string }
prompt.role: senior full-stack TypeScript engineer building a serverless multi-tenant CDP automation engine + Preact SPA, test-first
prompt.task: Implement "Triggers & enrollment: segment-entry + event + manual" to satisfy its test plan and acceptance criteria. Red → green → refactor. REUSE existing infra; do not reinvent.

title: Implement: Triggers & enrollment: segment-entry + event + manual (iteration 1)

criteria: Segment-entry enrollment still works (no regression). Event ingestion of the configured type enrolls the profile into the active automation exactly once (idempotent on replay); a non-matching event/filter does NOT enroll.

The manual/API enroll endpoint enrolls a profile (and a segment snapshot) at the start node, is capability-gated + workspace-scoped, and refuses a cross-workspace automation/profile/segment id (404/inv.2).
The re-enrollment policy is documented + enforced for all three trigger kinds. All verified against REAL Postgres; everything workspace-scoped.
phase.refs: §9B (enrollment), CLAUDE.md (segment_change_log → enrollFromSegmentChange; ingest/processor), the re-enrollment policy
scope: Persist + validate the TRIGGER definition for all three kinds: segment_entry (automations.trigger_segment_id, already wired via enrollFromSegmentChange), event (event type + optional payload filter — store in the trigger node/definition), manual (no auto-source).

EVENT-trigger enrollment: wire processor-time enrollment — when an event of the configured type (matching the optional filter) is ingested for a profile, enroll into the matching active automations (insert automation_enrollments at the start node, UNIQUE(automation_id, profile_id), ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, workspace-scoped). Idempotent (a replayed event enrolls at most once).

MANUAL/API enrollment: a workspace-scoped endpoint (e.g. POST /automations/:id/enroll) to enroll a single profile or a segment snapshot; capability-gated (manage_content); rejects cross-workspace ids (inv.2). Define + enforce the re-enrollment policy (once vs re-enter) consistently across all three kinds.
phase.title: Triggers & enrollment: segment-entry + event + manual

implUnits: 1. dsl.ts: extend TriggerNode for kind:'event' with `eventType: string` (required) + optional `filter?: AstNode` payload filter; manual stays {kind,next}; segment_entry unchanged. Extend validateNodeFields trigger case to require eventType for event triggers and to structurally check an optional filter. (No migration — event config lives in definition JSON.)

2. core.ts: add parseEventEnrollmentTrigger (event analogue of parseEnrollmentTrigger) + evaluateEventPayloadFilter (pure filter eval). Reuse decideReenrollment + buildEnrollmentInsert unchanged. Add an EventRow type (workspace_id, profile_id, type, payload, event_id).

3. enroll.ts: add enrollFromEvent(deps, eventRow) — reads active automations in the event's workspace whose definition has an event trigger matching the type (load definition, validate, resolveStartNode, read eventType+filter from the trigger node), evaluate the optional filter against the payload, build intents (parseEventEnrollmentTrigger), and apply buildEnrollmentInsert statements in ONE workspace-scoped tx — mirrors enrollFromSegmentChange exactly (in-code workspace scoping, ON CONFLICT 'once').

4. enroll.ts: add enrollProfileManually(deps, {workspaceId, automationId, profileId}) and enrollSegmentSnapshot(deps, {workspaceId, automationId, segmentId}) — resolve startNode from the automation's validated definition; the segment snapshot resolves current members (dynamic via @cdp/segments buildSegmentMatch/compileWhere; manual via segment_memberships) and inserts one enrollment per member; all workspace-scoped, ON CONFLICT 'once'.
5. index.ts: export enrollFromEvent, enrollProfileManually, enrollSegmentSnapshot, parseEventEnrollmentTrigger, evaluateEventPayloadFilter, and the new EventRow/TriggerNode types.

6. local-api handlers.ts: add enrollIntoAutomation handler for POST /automations/:id/enroll — validate body (exactly one of profile_id|segment_id), resolve the automation scoped to ctx.workspaceId (404 if absent), verify the profile/segment exists in ctx.workspaceId (404 cross-workspace), then call the automation-runner enroll core via an injected Reader+runInWorkspaceTx bound to the request pool (reuse the recomputeFeaturesAndSegments dependency-wiring style). Return enrolled count.

7. local-api handlers.ts: wire EVENT-trigger enrollment into the event landing path. In sendProfileEvent (and document the prod processor equivalent in services/processor/src/deps.ts.runSegmentReevalInTx neighbor), AFTER the event row is written + recomputeFeaturesAndSegments, call enrollFromEvent with the just-recorded event (type+payload+profile) on the SAME tx client. This is the local hook point that mirrors the processor; keep it idempotent and non-throwing.
8. local-api routes.ts: register 'POST /automations/:id/enroll' : 'manage_content'.
9. local-api handlers.ts handler map: add 'POST /automations/:id/enroll' : enrollIntoAutomation.

10. CLAUDE.md: document the re-enrollment policy (default 'once' across all three kinds; structural ON CONFLICT(automation_id,profile_id) guard) + the event-trigger config location (definition trigger node: eventType + optional payload filter) + the new endpoint. Bump root package.json (minor — feature).

11. (no migration) — if a 'once'/'always' policy ever needs persistence it would be a automations column; THIS phase keeps the default 'once' in code (decideReenrollment) so NO migration. If the design review insists on persisting the policy, add a automations.reenrollment_policy column and apply to BOTH cdp AND cdp_e2e via a node pg script (psql unavailable) — flagged, not assumed.
mocks: NONE for the DB tier — integration tests use REAL local Postgres (DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp) via adminPool()/hasDatabaseUrl(), with describe.skipIf(!RUN) and unique per-file workspace/automation/segment/profile UUID namespaces (parity with enrollment-from-segment-entry.integration.test.ts).
NO SES/SQS/Dispatcher in this phase — enrollment writes automation_enrollments only; it does NOT enqueue sends, so no aws-sdk-client-mock / LocalStack needed here.
Reader + runInWorkspaceTx are INJECTED (EnrollDeps), bound to the test's admin pool via runStatementsInWorkspaceTx — same dependency-injection seam the segment-entry test uses; no Postgres mock.
The local-api endpoint test drives the handler through the real app/router with a manage_content ctx; no AWS mocks.
plan.notes: WHERE EVENT-TRIGGER ENROLLMENT HOOKS IN: events land in the events table in two places that share semantics — (a) the production processor (services/processor: planProcessing → runPlanInWorkspaceTx, which after the event/feature upsert runs runSegmentReevalInTx on the open tx client), and (b) the local dev path POST /profiles/:id/events (sendProfileEvent → recomputeFeaturesAndSegments) which is the dev mirror (the local-api has no SQS/processor). The event-trigger enroll call (enrollFromEvent) should fire AT THE SAME POINT the segment re-eval fires — right after the event row + feature recompute, on the SAME workspace-scoped tx client (no nested BEGIN/COMMIT). For the dev tier that's inside sendProfileEvent; for prod it's the processor deps tx. IMPORTANT: enrollFromSegmentChange is currently EXPORTED but NOT yet wired into any live flow (only covered by tests) — so the segment-entry trigger is materialized but not auto-invoked at processor time today; this phase should NOT regress that (keep its tests green) and may optionally wire it alongside the event hook, but the criteria only require event + manual to be wired. RE-ENROLLMENT POLICY (recommended): default 'once' for ALL THREE kinds, enforced structurally by UNIQUE(automation_id, profile_id) + INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (buildEnrollmentInsert) and decided in code by decideReenrollment(hasExisting,'once'); 'always' (re-entry) stays a forward-compat enum value but is NOT enabled this phase. This is consistent because segment_entry, event, and manual ALL funnel through buildEnrollmentInsert. MIGRATION: NONE required — the event trigger's eventType + optional payload filter live in the automation definition JSON (the TriggerNode), and the 'once' policy is a code default; automation_enrollments already has UNIQUE(automation_id, profile_id). If a future design review wants the policy persisted per-automation, that single column (automations.reenrollment_policy default 'once') is the only migration and MUST be applied to BOTH cdp and cdp_e2e via a node pg script (psql unavailable) — flagged but out of scope. TENANT ISOLATION: every read filters WHERE workspace_id=$1 and every enrollment insert binds workspace_id at $1 (service role bypasses RLS); the endpoint takes workspace_id from the authorizer ctx ONLY and refuses cross-workspace automation/profile/segment ids with 404 (inv.2). The manual-enroll SEGMENT SNAPSHOT is point-in-time: resolve current members at call time (dynamic via buildSegmentMatch, manual via segment_memberships) and insert one enrollment each — later segment changes do not retroactively enroll. No sends are emitted in this phase (enrollment only), so no SES/SQS mocking needed. Follow process hygiene: bump root package.json (minor), update CLAUDE.md, restart dev API + rebuild changed packages after edits.
pureFunctions: parseEventEnrollmentTrigger(eventRow, automationTriggerRows) → EnrollmentIntent[] — event-kind analogue of parseEnrollmentTrigger; matches eventType, filters by workspace_id, drops intents whose payload-filter result is false (added to services/automation-runner/src/core.ts).

evaluateEventPayloadFilter(filter | undefined, payload) → boolean — pure, in-memory, closed-grammar evaluation of the optional event trigger filter against the event payload (payload.* namespace; reuses @cdp/segments operator/field whitelist conventions; no DB, never interpolated). In core.ts or a small filter module.
decideReenrollment(hasExisting, policy='once') → boolean — ALREADY EXISTS in core.ts; reused unchanged as the single re-enrollment decision for all three kinds.
buildEnrollmentInsert(ws, automation, profile, startNode) → SqlStatement — ALREADY EXISTS; the structural ON CONFLICT 'once' guard shared by segment_entry / event / manual.

validateAutomationDefinition (extended) — add event-trigger field validation (required eventType string; optional well-formed payload-filter AstNode) inside the existing trigger case of validateNodeFields in dsl.ts; no new public function.
resolveStartNode / parseEnrollmentTrigger — ALREADY EXIST; reused by the event + manual enroll orchestrators to compute startNode and shape intents.
reuse: enrollFromSegmentChange (enroll.ts) — the EXACT pattern to extend for enrollFromEvent: read active automations scoped to the workspace, validate+resolveStartNode, build intents, apply in one workspace-scoped tx. Same EnrollDeps { reader, runInWorkspaceTx } seam.
parseEnrollmentTrigger + EnrollmentIntent + AutomationTriggerRow (core.ts) — copy the shape for parseEventEnrollmentTrigger.
decideReenrollment + buildEnrollmentInsert (core.ts) — the single re-enrollment decision + structural ON CONFLICT 'once' insert, shared verbatim across all three trigger kinds.
validateAutomationDefinition / validateNodeFields / resolveStartNode (dsl.ts) — extend the trigger case; reuse the structural gate so the event fields ride the existing cycle/orphan/edge checks.

@cdp/segments buildSegmentMatch + compileWhere (statements.ts/compile.ts) — resolve the manual-enroll SEGMENT SNAPSHOT members (dynamic) parameterized with workspace_id at $1; segment_memberships query for manual segments (parity with listProfiles' segment branch in handlers.ts).
@cdp/segments AstNode + operator/field whitelist conventions — reuse for the event payload filter grammar (payload.* namespace) so it is compiled/whitelisted, never interpolated (invariant 6 spirit).

runStatementsInWorkspaceTx / withWorkspaceTx (automation-runner deps.ts) — the workspace-scoped tx runner the integration tests bind to adminPool(); asserts each statement is scoped to the requested workspace.

sendProfileEvent + recomputeFeaturesAndSegments (local-api handlers.ts) — the event landing path + the in-tx injected {reader, runInWorkspaceTx} wiring; the event-trigger enroll hook plugs in right after, on the same client.
scopedQuery (@cdp/db) + the validateSenderId cross-workspace-refusal pattern (handlers.ts) — for the endpoint's workspace scoping and 404-on-foreign-id behavior.
adminPool()/hasDatabaseUrl()/describe.skipIf + unique-UUID-namespace harness (enrollment-from-segment-entry.integration.test.ts) — the integration test scaffold to copy.

assertions: validateAutomationDefinition ACCEPTS a definition whose single trigger is { type:'trigger', kind:'event', eventType:'purchase', next:'x' } with no filter.
validateAutomationDefinition ACCEPTS an event trigger with an optional payload filter (an AstNode / rule object) and a valid eventType string.
validateAutomationDefinition THROWS when kind:'event' has a missing/empty eventType (event triggers MUST name the event type).
validateAutomationDefinition THROWS when the optional event-trigger filter is present but malformed (not an object / not a valid AstNode shape).

validateAutomationDefinition ACCEPTS kind:'manual' with only { kind, next } (no auto-source fields) and ACCEPTS kind:'segment_entry' unchanged (segment lives in automations.trigger_segment_id, NOT on the node) — segment_entry regression guard.

the exactly-ONE-trigger / resolvable-edges / no-cycle / no-orphan invariants are unaffected by the new event fields (a definition that was valid before stays valid; reuses existing detectCycle/hasReachableExit/findOrphans paths).
testFiles.criterion: Persist + validate the trigger definition for all three kinds (segment_entry | event | manual). The event trigger node must carry an event type + optional payload filter; manual carries no auto-source; the structural gate (validateAutomationDefinition) still passes/fails correctly.

assertions: parseEventEnrollmentTrigger(eventRow, automationTriggerRows) returns one EnrollmentIntent per active automation whose event trigger's eventType === the event's type (same workspace) — mirrors parseEnrollmentTrigger's shape (workspaceId/automationId/profileId/startNode).
an event type that matches NO automation's eventType yields zero intents.
a automation whose workspace_id !== the event row's workspace_id is filtered out (in-code tenant isolation; never enrolls cross-workspace).
when the event trigger has NO payload filter, a matching-type event always produces an intent.

when the event trigger HAS a payload filter, the intent is produced ONLY when matchesFilter===true is fed in (parse layer takes the boolean; the actual SQL match is the integration test's job) — a false filter result drops the intent.
THROWS on a falsy workspace_id (tenant-isolation guard, parity with parseEnrollmentTrigger).
testFiles.criterion: Event ingestion of the configured type (matching an optional payload filter) enrolls the profile into matching active automations exactly once; a non-matching event/filter does NOT enroll. (Pure parse layer — the SQL/idempotency is exercised in the integration test.)

assertions: evaluateEventPayloadFilter(undefined, anyPayload) === true (no filter ⇒ always matches).
a simple equality rule (e.g. payload.plan == 'pro') returns true for { plan:'pro' } and false for { plan:'free' } / a missing key.
numeric comparison (e.g. payload.amount >= 100) honors >= / < etc. against the event payload values.

an unknown/unsupported operator or a non-whitelisted path is REJECTED (throws) rather than silently matching — the filter is a closed grammar (reuses the @cdp/segments operator/field whitelist conventions; payload.* is the namespace).
the function is PURE and in-memory over the event payload object (no DB) so it is unit-testable without Postgres.
testFiles.criterion: The optional payload filter is evaluated against the INGESTED EVENT (not the profile), deterministically and safely (whitelist/parameterized — never interpolated).

assertions: decideReenrollment(hasExisting=false, 'once') === true; decideReenrollment(true, 'once') === false (default 'once').
decideReenrollment(true, 'always') === true; decideReenrollment(false, 'always') === true.

buildEnrollmentInsert always emits ON CONFLICT (automation_id, profile_id) DO NOTHING — the structural 'once' guard is identical regardless of trigger kind (segment_entry / event / manual all go through buildEnrollmentInsert).

the policy default is 'once' for ALL three kinds (assert the resolver/default used by enrollFromSegmentChange, the event-enroll path, and the manual-enroll path is the same decideReenrollment('once')).
testFiles.criterion: The re-enrollment policy is documented + enforced consistently across all three trigger kinds (decideReenrollment is the single decision point).

assertions: Given an ACTIVE automation with an event trigger (eventType='purchase', no filter), calling enrollFromEvent(deps, {workspace_id, profile_id, type:'purchase', payload, event_id}) inserts ONE automation_enrollments row at the start node with status='active' (current_node === definition.startNode).
REPLAY idempotency: invoking enrollFromEvent twice for the same (automation, profile) leaves exactly ONE enrollment row (ON CONFLICT (automation_id, profile_id) DO NOTHING) — count === 1.
an event of a DIFFERENT type than the automation's eventType enrolls NOBODY (0 rows for a fresh profile).

with a payload filter (payload.amount >= 100): a 'purchase' with amount=150 enrolls; a 'purchase' with amount=50 does NOT enroll (filter evaluated against the event payload; SQL/in-memory parameterized, never interpolated).

TENANT ISOLATION: a automation in workspace B with the same eventType is NOT enrolled when the event row belongs to workspace A; only workspace-A automations are read (WHERE workspace_id=$1) and every write binds workspace_id at $1.
an event trigger automation whose status != 'active' (draft/paused) does NOT enroll.
SEGMENT-ENTRY REGRESSION: in the SAME suite (or a shared harness), enrollFromSegmentChange on an 'entered' row still enrolls exactly once — assert the segment-entry path is untouched.

all three kinds share buildEnrollmentInsert → a profile already enrolled via segment_entry is NOT re-enrolled by a later matching event (single UNIQUE(automation_id,profile_id) row), proving the 'once' policy is consistent across kinds.
testFiles.criterion: Event ingestion of the configured type (matching the optional payload filter) enrolls the profile into matching active automations EXACTLY once (idempotent on replay); a non-matching event/filter does NOT enroll; segment-entry still works (no regression). Real Postgres, workspace-scoped.

assertions: Recording an event via the production processor-style path (the event row written + the post-event hook) invokes enrollFromEvent and produces the enrollment — proving the hook fires after the event lands (events row exists AND enrollment row exists).
the hook runs in/with the SAME workspace-scoped tx semantics as the existing feature/segment re-eval (no nested BEGIN/COMMIT against an open client; statements bind workspace_id at $1).

a replayed event_id (ON CONFLICT(event_id) DO NOTHING in the event insert) does NOT cause a second enrollment — the enroll hook is idempotent even when the underlying event insert was a no-op (assert enrollment count stays 1).
an event that matches no event-trigger automation leaves automation_enrollments empty (hook is a no-op, never throws, never blocks the event write).
testFiles.criterion: Event-trigger enrollment is wired at processor/ingest time: when an event of the configured type is recorded for a profile (the SAME code path POST /profiles/:id/events and the processor use), the profile is enrolled into matching active automations in the same workspace-scoped flow. Real Postgres.

assertions: POST /automations/:id/enroll { profile_id } enrolls that profile at the automation's start node (current_node === startNode, status 'active'); response reports enrolled count 1.

POST /automations/:id/enroll { segment_id } enrolls a SNAPSHOT of the segment's current members (each at the start node) — assert N enrollments for an N-member segment; the snapshot is resolved server-side (dynamic via compileWhere/buildSegmentMatch, manual via segment_memberships) and is point-in-time (a profile added to the segment AFTER the call is NOT enrolled).
IDEMPOTENT: a second identical enroll call inserts no duplicates (ON CONFLICT (automation_id, profile_id) DO NOTHING) — re-enroll a 3-member snapshot ⇒ still 3 rows.
CROSS-WORKSPACE automation id (a automation in another workspace): 404 (scopedQuery resolves nothing in the token's workspace) — no enrollment written.
CROSS-WORKSPACE profile_id: 404/refused (the profile must exist in ctx.workspaceId, mirrors sendProfileEvent's present-check) — never enrolls another tenant's profile.
CROSS-WORKSPACE segment_id: 404/refused (segment resolved scoped to ctx.workspaceId; a foreign id resolves to nothing) — inv.2.
workspace_id is taken from ctx (authorizer claim) ONLY — a workspace_id in the request body is ignored (assert a body workspace_id does not leak into the write).
CAPABILITY GATE: the route is registered as 'POST /automations/:id/enroll' : 'manage_content' in routes.ts (assert the entry exists / a non-manage_content token is rejected by the gate).
the inserted enrollment's workspace_id === ctx.workspaceId and automation_id === the path id (every write binds workspace_id at $1).
testFiles.criterion: The manual/API enroll endpoint (POST /automations/:id/enroll) enrolls a single profile OR a segment snapshot at the start node, is capability-gated (manage_content) + workspace-scoped, and refuses cross-workspace automation/profile/segment ids (404/inv.2). Real Postgres.

assertions: missing both profile_id and segment_id ⇒ 400 (a target is required).
both profile_id AND segment_id supplied ⇒ 400 (ambiguous; choose one).
a non-string/blank profile_id or segment_id ⇒ 400.
the handler RETURNS the promise so the kit Button auto-lock contract holds (no implementation detail leaked — assert via the handler signature/return, consistent with existing handlers).
testFiles.criterion: The manual-enroll handler input contract: exactly one of profile_id | segment_id required; bad input rejected before any DB write.
